SEI: The presence of BNNSs in BN-QSSE does not fundamentally alter the SEI components compared to QSSE, as indicated by TOF-SIMS analysis showing similar C2HO− and NaO− signals, but it improves interfacial chemistry by accelerating and homogenizing Na+ ion transport and suppressing Na dendrites due to high Young's modulus.

CEI: NaDFOB forms a NaF and boride-rich inorganic solid electrolyte interphase (CEI) that is thinner, denser, and provides stronger protection for the cathode, inhibiting transition metal dissolution and improving stability.
High_voltage_performance_CEI: The electrolyte with NaDFOB exhibits higher oxidation activity, initiating at approximately 4.25 V, and improves capacity retention; for example, in Na || NFM111 cells, it shows 81.44% capacity retention after 200 cycles at 1 C, compared to 64.40% for the base electrolyte.
SEI: NaDFOB forms a NaF and boride-rich SEI with good electronic insulation and high mechanical strength, suppressing solvent decomposition, reducing interfacial resistance, inhibiting sodium dendrite growth, and promoting uniform sodium deposition.
Optimal_operating_temperature: The BE-NaDFOB electrolyte is capable of stable operation in Na || NFM111 batteries from -40°C to 60°C, as demonstrated in cycling tests at these temperatures.

CEI: The boron-containing additive NaDFOB promotes the formation of a thin, dense, and stable cathode/electrolyte interphase (CEI) enriched with inorganic boron and phosphorus compounds, which suppresses parasitic reactions and facilitates Na+ transport.
High_voltage_performance_CEI: The battery with the additive exhibits stable cycling performance at 4.2V, with a discharge capacity over 165 mAh g⁻¹ and high capacity retention of 85.2% after 800 cycles at 1C, compared to rapid failure in electrolytes without such additives.
